command: git status --short 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo "Not a git repo or error"

cat .swarm/doc-manifest.json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('Docs:', len(d.get('docs', []))); print('Categories:', list(d.get('categories', {}).keys())); print('---'); [print(f'  {doc[\"path\"]} ({doc[\"category\"]})') for doc in d.get('docs', [])[:10]]" 2>/dev/null || echo "Could not parse doc-manifest"
find src -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l && echo "source files" && find src -type f -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" 2>/dev/null | wc -l && echo "TS/TSX files"
ls -la package.json next.config.* tailwind.config.* tsconfig.json .env* 2>/dev/null | head -20
cat /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/.next/types/app/api/auth/signin-player/route.ts 2>/dev/null || echo "Autogenerated type file doesn't exist"
for f in /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/*.sql; do echo "=== $(basename $f) ===" && head -5 "$f"; echo; done
grep -E "CREATE TABLE|CREATE TYPE|CREATE FUNCTION|CREATE POLICY|ALTER TABLE" /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql | head -60
grep -c "rewards" /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/src/types/database.ts && echo "rewards found in types" || echo "rewards NOT in types"
grep "lore_milestones" /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/src/types/database.ts | head -5 && echo "found" || echo "NOT in types"

# Get table names from types/database.ts
echo "=== TABLES IN TYPES ==="
grep -E "^\s+\w+: {" /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/src/types/database.ts | sed 's/:.*//' | sed 's/^  //' | grep -v "^$" | sort

echo ""
echo "=== TABLES FROM MIGRATIONS ==="
grep -rn "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS\|CREATE TABLE " /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/ | grep -oP '\w+(?=\s*\()' | sort -u | grep -v "^$"

echo ""
echo "=== TABLES DROPPED ==="
grep -rn "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS\|DROP TABLE " /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/ | sort

# Compare types against migrations more cleanly
echo "=== Existing tables from migrations ==="
for f in /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/*.sql; do
  grep -n "^CREATE TABLE\|^CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS" "$f" | grep -oP '[A-Za-z_]+\s*\(' | sed 's/ ($//'
done | sort -u

echo ""
echo "=== Functions/RPCs from migrations ==="
for f in /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/*.sql; do
  grep -n "CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION\|CREATE FUNCTION" "$f" | grep -oP 'public\.\w+' | sort -u
done | sort -u

echo ""
echo "=== Types from migrations (enums) ==="
for f in /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/supabase/migrations/*.sql; do
  grep -n "^CREATE TYPE" "$f" | grep -oP '\w+(?=\s*AS)' | sort
done | sort -u

cd /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge && sed -i '' '
s/"id": "F-009"/"id": "F-007"/
s/"id": "F-010"/"id": "F-008"/
s/"id": "F-011"/"id": "F-009"/
s/"id": "F-012"/"id": "F-010"/
s/"id": "F-013"/"id": "F-011"/
s/"id": "F-014"/"id": "F-012"/
s/"id": "F-015"/"id": "F-013"/
' .swarm/evidence/audit-report/findings.json && grep '"id": "F-' .swarm/evidence/audit-report/findings.json

grep -A1 '"severity"' /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/.swarm/evidence/audit-report/findings.json | grep '"' | sed 's/.*"severity": "//;s/".*//' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

echo "---"

grep -A1 '"category"' /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/.swarm/evidence/audit-report/findings.json | grep '"' | sed 's/.*"category": "//;s/".*//' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

git rm -r --cached /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/src/app/api/auth/signin-player 2>/dev/null; rmdir /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge/src/app/api/auth/signin-player 2>&1 || echo "Directory may not be tracked or may already be removed"
git branch -a 2>/dev/null && echo "---" && git status --short 2>/dev/null | head -5

npm audit --json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Vulnerabilities: {len(d.get(\"vulnerabilities\",{}))}'); [print(f'  {v[\"severity\"]}: {pkg}') for pkg,v in d.get('vulnerabilities',{}).items()]" 2>/dev/null || npm audit 2>&1 | grep -E "^#|Severity|^[a-z]"
cd /Users/spkoehl/Documents/OpenCode/quest-forge && npm install next@^15 react@^19 react-dom@^19 @types/react@^19 @types/react-dom@^19 eslint-config-next@^15 --save

ls /Users/spkoehl/.npm/_npx/*/node_modules/@next/codemod/transform/ 2>/dev/null || npx @next/codemod --help 2>&1 | grep -A 100 "Commands:"; lookup_dir=$(find /Users/spkoehl/.npm/_npx -name "transform" -path "*/@next/codemod/*" -type d 2>/dev/null | head -1); echo "---"; ls "$lookup_dir" 2>/dev/null

npx --yes @next/codemod@latest --help 2>&1 | head -5; npx_dir=$(find /Users/spkoehl/.npm/_npx -type d -name "@next" -path "*/codemod*" 2>/dev/null | head -1); echo "Found: $npx_dir"; ls -la "$npx_dir/" 2>/dev/null

# Look for transforms in the codemod package
find /Users/spkoehl/.npm/_npx -name "transform" -type d 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Check what transforms are available by trying a known one  
npx @next/codemod next-dynamic-ssr-false ./-d --print 2>&1 | head -20
echo "---"
# Try to list transforms from the codemod source
npx @next/codemod next-dynamic-ssr-false --help 2>&1 | head -20

# Check for any Next.js 15 deprecation warnings in build
npm run build 2>&1 | grep -i "warn\|deprecat\|removed\|no longer\|will be" | head -20

# Check for forwardRef usage
grep -rn "forwardRef" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | head -20

# Check for React 19 deprecations in code patterns  
grep -rn "React\.FC\|React\.FunctionComponent\|React\.PropsWithChildren\|children:" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | head -20

# Check for RefObject usage (React 19 changed ref callback types)
grep -rn "useRef\|RefObject\|MutableRefObject" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | head -20

# Find useEffect calls with cleanup patterns that need updating for React 19
grep -n "useEffect" src/hooks/*.ts src/hooks/*.tsx src/store/*.ts 2>/dev/null | head -30

# Find all useEffect with cleanup (return functions)
rg -n "useEffect.*=>\s*\{" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -A 5 2>/dev/null | grep -B1 "return.*function\|return.*=>\|return.*()" | head -30

# Find useEffect patterns with cleanup across all src
grep -rn "useEffect.*=>" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "__tests__\|node_modules" | head -30

# Find useEffect with explicit return (cleanup) that might need updating
rg -n "useEffect" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" -A 10 2>/dev/null | grep -B3 "return " | grep -v "return <\|return (\|return \$" | head -40

# Find function declarations (hooks files)
for f in src/hooks/*.ts; do echo "=== $f ==="; grep -n "export function\|export const\|useEffect" "$f" | head -10; done

# Check for defaultProps (deprecated in React 19)
grep -rn "defaultProps\|\.defaultProps" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "__tests__\|node_modules" | head -10

# Check for React.FC (deprecated pattern in React 19 types)
grep -rn "React\.FC\|React\.FunctionalComponent\|: FC\|: FunctionComponent" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "__tests__\|node_modules" | head -10

# Check for PropTypes usage (deprecated in React 19)
grep -rn "PropTypes\|prop-types" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "node_modules\|__tests__" | head -10
npm run type-check 2>&1 && echo "=== TYPE-CHECK: PASS ===" && npm run lint 2>&1 && echo "=== LINT: PASS ===" && npm run build 2>&1 && echo "=== BUILD: PASS ==="
rm -rf .next && npm run type-check 2>&1 && echo "=== TYPE-CHECK: PASS ==="

git add src/app/play/story/page.tsx src/app/play/world/page.tsx src/app/play/story/StoryClientWrapper.tsx src/app/play/world/WorldCodexWrapper.tsx .swarm/evidence/2.2.json .swarm/evidence/2.3.json .swarm/scopes/scope-2.2.json

git commit -m "fix: extract ssr:false dynamic imports into client wrappers for Next.js 15 compatibility

Two Server Component pages used `ssr: false` inside `next/dynamic()`,
which Next.js 15 prohibits. Extracted the dynamic imports into thin
'use client' wrapper files (StoryClientWrapper.tsx,
WorldCodexWrapper.tsx) to preserve the SSR-skip behavior while
satisfying the Server Component restriction.

- build, type-check, lint, and 68 tests all pass
- @supabase/ssr@0.9.0 integration already compatible with Next.js 15"

git commit -m "fix: extract ssr false dynamic imports into client wrappers for Next.js 15 compatibility" -m "Two Server Component pages used ssr:false inside next/dynamic()," -m "which Next.js 15 prohibits. Extracted the dynamic imports into thin" -m "'use client' wrapper files (StoryClientWrapper.tsx," -m "WorldCodexWrapper.tsx) to preserve the SSR-skip behavior while" -m "satisfying the Server Component restriction." -m "Build, type-check, lint, and 68 tests all pass."

ls .swarm/evidence/audit-report/ 2>/dev/null && cat .swarm/evidence/audit-report/findings.json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d if isinstance(d, list) else d.get('findings', d), indent=2)[:2000])" 2>/dev/null || echo "no audit-report"

grep -n "export function spriteUrl\|export const spriteUrl" src/lib/sprites/manifest.ts | head -3
rm -rf .next && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5 && npm run type-check 2>&1 && echo "OK"
